methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $105M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $1.5B - $4.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Aleph Farms is a Series C biotech company in the Biotech & Health sector that has raised $105M to date. Valuation analysis relies on a peer universe of 3 public comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers, with 3 same-stage private companies providing direct comparison: Ultima Genomics ($4.0B), Insitro ($2.4B), and Colossal Biosciences ($1.5B).

Two valuation methods proved applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using the $105M total raised as a proxy baseline and applying a 1.7x Series C step-up multiplier typical to the next funding round, implies a valuation of $178M. Peer-median analysis across the three same-sector, same-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $2.4B, with a range spanning $1.5B to $4.0B. Public-comp anchoring was not applicable given the absence of disclosed revenue for Aleph Farms.

The triangulated analysis across these two methods supports a valuation range of $152M (low) to $2.8B (high), with a median of $1.3B. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around whether stage-based progression or peer multiples better reflect market conditions for frontier biotech. Key unknowns include Aleph Farms' current revenue trajectory, capital efficiency relative to peers, and technology differentiation relative to the sample cohort.
